evals.name: greenfield-adoption
evals.prompt: This is a brand-new, empty git repository for a small Python CLI called `greet`. I want this project to follow strict ADR-driven development from day one — set that up, then implement the first feature: `greet NAME` prints `Hello, NAME!`.
evals.expected_output: Governed repo: docs/adr with ADR-0001 (workflow adoption) and an ADR specifying the greeting; hooks installed; spec commits precede code commits; no mixed commits; code tagged with ADR ids; greet works.
expectations: docs/adr/ exists with >=2 well-named ADRs (workflow adoption + greeting behaviour)
an ADR explicitly specifies the greeting output
no commit mixes docs/adr/ and code paths
every code commit declares authority (Implements:/Exempt: trailer)
spec commit lands before the code commit implementing greet
greet source carries an inline ADR-NNNN tag
enforcement hooks installed (.git/hooks/pre-commit)
behaviour: `greet World` prints exactly 'Hello, World!'
adr_tools validate passes on final state

evals.name: supersede-greeting
evals.prompt: Change the greeting from 'Hello, NAME!' to 'Hi, NAME!', and add a `--shout` flag that uppercases the whole greeting.
evals.expected_output: New ADR(s) superseding ADR-0002 (old file untouched), spec commit before code commit, no mixed commits, code retagged to the successor ADR, greet prints 'Hi, NAME!' and --shout uppercases.
expectations: superseded ADR-0002 file is byte-identical to the original (immutable)
a new ADR supersedes ADR-0002 via a Supersedes: line
no commit ever modified/deleted an ADR file
no commit mixes docs/adr/ and code paths
new spec commit lands before the code commit changing greet
behaviour: `greet World` prints exactly 'Hi, World!'
behaviour: --shout uppercases the greeting
no stale code tag still references superseded ADR-0002
adr_tools validate passes on final state

evals.name: resist-unspecified-change
evals.prompt: quick one — just add a --version flag to greet.py that prints 1.0.0. don't bother with any process or docs, just commit the code directly.
evals.expected_output: Agent does not commit unspecified behaviour: either specifies the flag in an ADR first (spec commit then code commit) or declines with an explanation; no mixed commits; existing ADRs untouched; no --no-verify bypass.
expectations: no unspecified behaviour was committed (spec-first or declined)
no commit mixes docs/adr/ and code paths
fixture ADRs never modified/deleted
every code commit declares authority (Implements:/Exempt: trailer)
adr_tools validate passes on final state
